title: Privilege Escalation: Sensitive Privileges Assigned to Non-Admin Account
description: Detects Event 4672 special privilege assignment with dangerous privileges (SeDebugPrivilege, SeTcbPrivilege, SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege) to accounts that aren't SYSTEM or known admin accounts — indicates privilege escalation or token manipulation.
query: 'Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' and 'Event ID' = '4672' and ('Original Log Content' like '%SeDebugPrivilege%' or 'Original Log Content' like '%SeTcbPrivilege%' or 'Original Log Content' like '%SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege%' or 'Original Log Content' like '%SeLoadDriverPrivilege%' or 'Original Log Content' like '%SeImpersonatePrivilege%') and not 'Original Log Content' like '%SubjectUserName:SYSTEM%' and not 'Original Log Content' like '%SubjectUserName:-$%' | eval Account = regexextract('Original Log Content', 'SubjectUserName:([^;]+)'), Domain = regexextract('Original Log Content', 'SubjectDomainName:([^;]+)') | stats count as Assignments, distinctcount(Entity) as Hosts by Account, Domain | sort -Assignments
falsepositives: Service accounts with legitimate elevated privileges
